named_technique: Slot-1318 Golomb a^2*b^3 + simultaneous Kronecker on (R/Z)^M via Besicovitch Q-LI of sqrt-primes
bridge_lemma: For each M, with beta_i := p_i^{-3/2} for the first M primes, Besicovitch Q-LI of sqrt-primes plus simultaneous Kronecker density on (R/Z)^M force infinitely many n at which fract(n*beta_i) > 1 - beta_i for all i, yielding M distinct powerful m_i := ceil(n*beta_i)^2 * p_i^3 in [n^2,(n+1)^2). Hence (using slot 1318 Golomb for powerful-ness and distinctness via Golomb uniqueness) h(n) >= M frequently for arbitrary M, so limsup h = infinity.
informal_proof_outline:
GOAL: Filter.atTop.limsup ((fun n => (erdos_942.h n : ENat))) = top, with h(n) := ((Finset.Ico (n^2) ((n+1)^2)).filter Nat.Powerful).card. STRATEGY: combine slot-1318 Golomb parametrization with simultaneous Diophantine approximation to force M distinct powerful integers into [n^2,(n+1)^2) for arbitrarily large M infinitely often.

L1 (slot 1318, USE) powerful_of_sq_mul_cube: 1<=a, 1<=b, Squarefree b => Nat.Powerful (a^2*b^3). Proved.

L2 (slot 1318, USE) sq_mul_cube_of_powerful: 1<=n, Nat.Powerful n => exists a b, 1<=a, 1<=b, Squarefree b, n=a^2*b^3. Proved; uniqueness via per-prime exponent profile.

L3 (MATHLIB GAP, AXIOM) L_LI_sqrt_primes: for injective primes p : Fin M -> Nat, the vector Fin.cons (1:R) (fun i => (p i : R)^((3:R)/2)) is LinearIndependent over Q. Proof: Besicovitch 1940 gives Q-LI of {1, sqrt(p_i)}; rewrite p^{3/2} = p*sqrt(p); a relation q_0 + sum q_i*p_i*sqrt(p_i)=0 has rational coeffs on the Q-LI basis {1,sqrt(p_i)}, forcing q_0=0 and q_i*p_i=0 hence q_i=0. NOT IN MATHLIB4 — only irrational_sqrt_prime exists; the multi-prime compositum result is absent.

L4 (MATHLIB GAP, AXIOM) L_sim_Kronecker: for beta : Fin M -> R with LinearIndependent Q (Fin.cons (1:R) beta), any nonempty open U in (Fin M -> AddCircle (1:R)) is hit infinitely often by n |-> (fun i => ((n:R)*beta i)). Equivalent to density of (n*beta_i mod 1)_i in [0,1)^M. NOT IN MATHLIB4 — only denseRange_zsmul_iff (single variable, in Mathlib.Topology.Instances.AddCircle.DenseSubgroup) exists; no AddCircle^M / ergodic_pi / Weyl. Proof from primitives: induction on M, product topology, denseRange_zsmul_iff on each fiber, Q-LI to exclude non-trivial closed subgroups (character-orthogonality argument).

L5 (consequence of L3+L4) box_hit_infinite: with beta_i := (p_i)^{-3/2}, the set {n : Nat | Int.fract (n*beta_i) > 1 - beta_i for all i} is infinite. L_LI applies to beta after rational rescaling (beta_i = p_i^{-3/2} = p_i^{3/2}/p_i^3).

L6 (real-analysis + slot 1318) box_construction: for n in L5 with n >= (p (Fin.last M)::R)^((3:R)/2), let u_i := Nat.ceil (n*beta_i). Then u_i in [n*beta_i, (n+1)*beta_i), so u_i*p_i^{3/2} in [n,n+1), so u_i^2*p_i^3 in [n^2,(n+1)^2). Each m_i is powerful by L1 (p_i squarefree). Distinctness: m_i=m_j with i!=j contradicts L2 Golomb uniqueness (different squarefree b=p_i vs p_j).

L7 (counting) card_powerful_ge_of_box: the M distinct powerful m_i in [n^2,(n+1)^2) prove erdos_942.h n >= M.

MAIN: by L5+L6+L7, for every M, ((M:ENat) <= (h n : ENat)) holds Filter.Frequently atTop. By Filter.limsup_eq_top_iff_frequently_natCast (or analogous ENat lemma), atTop.limsup ((fun n => (h n : ENat))) = top. QED.

MATHLIB GAP DISCLOSURE: L3 (Besicovitch Q-LI of sqrt-primes) and L4 (simultaneous Kronecker on AddCircle^M) are axiomatized — substantial but elementary infrastructure missing from Mathlib4 (confirmed via web-search May 31 2026). Aristotle's MCGS + informal reasoner may autoformalize L3 from quadratic-extension compositum (Q-dim 2^M for Q(sqrt(p_1),...,sqrt(p_M))) and L4 from the inductive product-topology argument; otherwise the deliverable is the conditional implication (L3+L4 axiomatized) ==> erdos_942_variants_limsup_main, a clean Mathlib-PR-valuable Golomb-Kronecker reduction.

FALLBACK: if L4 too heavy, target the conditional. Alternative paths (Lindemann-Weierstrass powers of pi; sqrt(2)/cbrt(2) generators; direct sieve a la De Koninck-Luca-Shparlinski 2005) are all harder; Besicovitch + Kronecker is the cleanest known classical route.
candidate_lemmas: L1 (slot 1318, USE): powerful_of_sq_mul_cube : forall a b, 1 <= a -> 1 <= b -> Squarefree b -> Nat.Powerful (a^2 * b^3).

L2 (slot 1318, USE): sq_mul_cube_of_powerful : forall n, 1 <= n -> Nat.Powerful n -> exists a b, 1 <= a /\ 1 <= b /\ Squarefree b /\ n = a^2 * b^3. (Uniqueness follows from the per-prime profile in the proof, but only existence is needed here.)

L3 (MATHLIB GAP, AXIOM): L_LI_sqrt_primes : forall M, forall p : Fin M -> Nat, Function.Injective p -> (forall i, Nat.Prime (p i)) -> LinearIndependent Q (Fin.cons (1:R) (fun i => (p i : R) ^ ((3:R)/2))).

L4 (MATHLIB GAP, AXIOM): L_sim_Kronecker : forall M, forall beta : Fin M -> R, LinearIndependent Q (Fin.cons (1:R) beta) -> forall U : Set (Fin M -> AddCircle (1:R)), U.Nonempty -> IsOpen U -> {n : Nat | (fun i => ((n : R) * beta i : AddCircle (1:R))) in U}.Infinite.
L5 (consequence of L3+L4): box_hit_infinite : for M primes p, {n : Nat | for all i, Int.fract ((n : R) * (p i : R)^(-(3:R)/2)) > 1 - (p i : R)^(-(3:R)/2)}.Infinite.

L6 (Real-analysis bookkeeping with slot 1318 L1+L2): box_construction : for n in L5 with n >= (p (Fin.last M) : R)^((3:R)/2), exists u : Fin M -> Nat with all u_i >= 1 and u_i^2 * (p i)^3 in Finset.Ico (n^2) ((n+1)^2); the M values are distinct (L2 uniqueness).
L7 (Counting): card_powerful_ge_of_box : the M distinct powerful integers in [n^2,(n+1)^2) prove erdos_942.h n >= M.
Main (assemble): erdos_942_variants_limsup_main : Filter.atTop.limsup ((fun n => (erdos_942.h n : ENat))) = top, via Filter.limsup_eq_top_iff_frequently applied to L5+L6+L7.
